accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text, and contextual additions (DOE administration, IIJA statute, FOIA reference) are accurate and verifiable.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; identifying FOIA, FACA, and DOE as administering agency are accurate context, not hallucinations.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes and expands DOE's Energy Sector Operational Support for Cyberresilience Program to strengthen energy sector defense.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the bill amends Section 40125(c) of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and describes the added objectives and provisions.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures key conditions including sole/unreviewable discretion, no right or benefit, FACA non-applicability, and disclosure exemptions.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the extension of authorization from FY2022-2026 to FY2027-2031.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all essential operative effects: new objectives, ETAC establishment, discretion/no-benefit, FACA exemption, disclosure exemption, transaction authority, and reauthorization.

objective_tone.why: Tone is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, logically structured, and internally consistent.

concise.why: Efficient with no padding or repetition while remaining complete.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Department of Energy, Secretary, Energy Threat Analysis Center, FACA, FOIA, and the IIJA.

correct_figures.why: The fiscal year ranges (2022-2026 to 2027-2031) match the bill text exactly.
